context: Verify that the agent correctly identifies memv as a borderline tool (primary focus is long-term memory storage, but it surfaces retrieved episodes into context), documents the borderline classification with reasoning, and still produces a useful reference summary.

checklist.name: Scope assessed before writing the summary
checklist.description: The agent evaluates the tool's scope against the in scope / borderline / out of scope criteria before creating references/<slug>.md.

checklist.name: Tool classified as borderline, not in scope or out of scope
checklist.description: The scope-fit field in the reference file and/or REVIEWED.md shows 'borderline'. It is not classified as simply 'in scope' or 'out of scope'.

checklist.name: Borderline reasoning explains the out-of-scope aspect
checklist.description: The reasoning notes that memv's primary function is long-term memory storage (episode extraction, bi-temporal validity) — which is primarily an agentic-memory concern.

checklist.name: Borderline reasoning explains the in-scope aspect
checklist.description: The reasoning notes that memv surfaces retrieved episodes into agent context at query time (retrieval-augmented context injection), which overlaps with this repo's scope.

checklist.name: Context-layer overlap specifically described in the reference file
checklist.description: The architecture overview or open questions section in references/<slug>.md specifically addresses the context injection / retrieval-to-context pathway, not just the storage layer.

checklist.name: references/<slug>.md is created despite borderline classification
checklist.description: The agent creates a reference summary even though the tool is borderline — a borderline result is triaged, not silently skipped.

checklist.name: REVIEWED.md scope-fit field shows borderline with reason
checklist.description: The detailed REVIEWED.md section includes scope-fit: 'borderline — <explanation>' rather than a binary in/out classification.

checklist.name: REVIEWED.md disposition is pending, not out of scope
checklist.description: The disposition field in REVIEWED.md is 'pending', leaving the promotion decision to the user rather than auto-rejecting.

checklist.name: User is informed of the borderline classification
checklist.description: The agent explicitly tells the user that the tool is borderline and explains why, so the user can make an informed decision.

checklist.name: Tool is not rejected without assessing the context layer
checklist.description: The agent does not dismiss the tool as out of scope based solely on its memory-storage focus without first examining whether it has a context-injection component.
